issue_title: Error "https://svelte.dev/e/each_key_duplicate" due to duplicate balance directive
issue_body:
I upgraded from V1.30.6 to V1.30.12. If while using V1.30.6 I accidentally created a duplicate balance directive, the errors page correctly showed the duplicate directive error and the corresponding line number in the beancount file. With V1.30.12, the errors page only show the svelte error in the title.

I've attached a sample file if it helps.

[Test.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25940062/Test.txt)

issue_title: Error https://svelte.dev/e/each_key_duplicate on Fava  import page
issue_body:
Hi!

I have recently migrated all my importers to V3/beangulp .
The importers works fine in CLI.


Since then, i have an error in Fava on the /import/ page:

```
Loading /comptes-famille-xxxx/import/ failed with error:
https://svelte.dev/e/each_key_duplicate
```

I don't have any additional error in the browser console.
I don't have any additional message on the Favai Command line error output.


Can you point me in the right direction ?
issue_comments:
Ok,
I have found the issue. 
The problem was multiple instance of the directives:
custom "fava-option" "import-config"
(I had one in each of my .beancount files)

I am not sure if this was an expected behaviour, but after removing all the duplicates directives, the issue is solved.

(Credits to Gemini for pointing me in the right direction)

Thanks for the report and I'm glad you got it work again. We should still handle this better without having the page error.

> The problem was multiple instance of the directives:
custom "fava-option" "import-config"
(I had one in each of my .beancount files)

I cannot explain how this option would have that effect - did you maybe also have duplicate (or overlapping, e.g. one being a subdirectory of another) import directories?
All the custom "fava-option" "import-config" were pointing to the same directory.

issue_title: Regression in v1.30.3-6-g191efcdd refactor: use keys for svelte {#each} blocks
issue_body:
This commit introduced a regression in the account page when you select the "Changes (monthly)" or "Balances (monthly)" links, where it shows the correct column headings but only one column of numbers.

I performed a manual git bisect between v1.30.3 which works, and v1.30.4 which doesn't, and identified this as the offending commit:

```
commit 191efcddf3d52122288fb5a49014cc16d6a87e91
Author: Jakob Schnitzer <mail@jakobschnitzer.de>
Date:   Thu May 22 08:54:47 2025 +0200

    refactor: use keys for svelte {#each} blocks
```
issue_comments: Thanks for the report and for bisecting it :)

issue_title: Cursor jumps in "Add transaction" posting fields
issue_body:
When adding a transaction via the "Add transaction" dialog, the cursor will jump to the end of the text when trying to edit the name or amount of a posting.

## Steps to reproduce:

1. Open the "Add transaction" dialog
2. Fill out a posting line (e.g. by getting it pre-filled from selecting a known payee)
3. Attempt to edit the name or amount
4. On every change, the cursor will jump to the end, making it very annoying to change more than one character

## Versions

Fava: 1.30.3
Firefox: 138.0.4
issue_comments: Thanks for reporting this bug. Seems to be some odd issue with the `{#each}` rendering in svelte, but should be fixed by linked PR.

issue_title: `/statement` does not normalize file paths
issue_body:
The `fava.plugins.link_documents` plugin properly normalizes paths so that documents can be referenced with relative paths: https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/bb37f983bcda81acaa04c607a342fad7a85eedd1/src/fava/plugins/link_documents.py#L76

However, the `/statement` endpoint doesn't perform the same normalization: https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/bb37f983bcda81acaa04c607a342fad7a85eedd1/src/fava/core/__init__.py#L612-L618

This results in navigating to a linked document from a journal entry failing.

@egetzner can you provide some example to reproduce this with and create a new issue? I assume your importer is returning an unexpected object type in one of its methods - have you checked that it follows the importer protocol?

@yagebu looks the type error resulted from an incorrect importer name resolution for the Importer classes:
In my old ImporterProtocol code, I would override the name method like so:
```
    def name(self):
        return f'{self.IMPORTER_NAME}: ({self.importer_account})'
```
Since the new Importer defines name as a property instead of a method, this didn't fail directly, the name just looked strange in the beangulp identify command:
`  <bound method Importer.name of <shared.erste.Importer object at 0x7be988fb0830>> `

Fava couldn't resolve the name once it tried to print it in the importer page. Changing the name to a property fixed this issue for me.
```
    @property
    def name(self):
        return f'{self.IMPORTER_NAME}: ({self.importer_account})'
```

Thanks for your response!

Good that you got it working - the name method / now property would have been my first guess as well :) I've runtime validations of these types returned by the various methods in #1931 so that others who run into this sort of issue should get a better error message.

issue_title: Cursor jumps on save in editor
issue_body:
I've been using `fava` quite a lot, and one thing has annoyed me quite a bit: each time I save, the cursor jumps to the end of the file, or near the `insert-entry` location.

Is there a reason why, when saving, the cursor couldn't be restored to where it was, and the view port would just stay where it was?

Am I the only one with this issue?

thanks! awesome project btw.
issue_comments: This has been driving me crazy lately too.  My files are not chronologically ordered, rather they are broken into multiple 'sections' by transaction type, and then ordered chronologically within that section.  When I save interim work within that section, it moves to the end of the file and I have to move back to that section.
Same with me. But I fixed it by [this issue comment](https://github.com/beancount/fava/issues/1393#issuecomment-1055081416)

issue_title: Unconsistency in week Date filter
issue_body:
The week filter is not consistent, there is a 1 week offset when appling a date filter.
For example if you rund the [DEMO](https://fava.pythonanywhere.com/example-beancount-file/income_statement/). 

Show the Expenses, and apply the daily interval, with the changes(weekly) display, the expenses are shown per week, as expected. 

![image](https://github.com/beancount/fava/assets/151871234/5e55a81f-27c8-4483-ac25-6d3e33fcef1f)

If you click on the 2017-W36 week, there will be no data shown, to show the 2017-W36 expenses you need to filter the date to 2017-W35.

I believe the proper week is 2017-W36 so there is a -1 week difference in the week date filter?
issue_comments:
In fava/src/fava/util/date.py
there is the following function:
![image](https://github.com/beancount/fava/assets/151871234/e5bd559b-1827-46c3-919c-2c9aa10ccf42)
There is a +1 in the date defining, so if a date is ISO week no. 1 it will return week 2.
So it seem to be a wanted behavious, but fava on the display use the standard ISO week format, thus the inconsistency?

Test did not pass, confirming this is a wanted behaviour, so maybe we should change the week number display on weekly expenses display?
![image](https://github.com/beancount/fava/assets/151871234/362ca1da-853b-438d-ba77-3c83ee7a1313)

> Test did not pass, confirming this is a wanted behaviour

That's not necessarily the case - tests are like code, they have bugs too ;) The wanted behaviour here would be for frontend and backend to match up of course. IMHO both should just match what strftime does for `%W` (here's how the Python docs describe that: "Week number of the year (Monday as the first day of the week) as a zero-padded decimal number. All days in a new year preceding the first Monday are considered to be in week 0.".) - the frontend does that using d3-time-format, we should change the Python side to match.
Thanks for the info, I will double check the d3 to confirm it does the same as python fromisocalendar regarding week handling and summit an updated pull request afterwards.

You can have a look at the updated #1807 let me know what you think.
I believe the python week and d3 would align perfectly with this. 

Side note, in general those who cares about week numbers are mostly european using ISO format for week number, in america we rarely use week number. You can have a read with [this here](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/oldnewthing/20160311-00/?p=93144) it makes a lot of sense. So I wonder if eventually switching week numbering to ISO could be a better solution?

issue_title: Fava freezes on request inside Docker container (only!)
issue_body:
I have only been able to reproduce this when running Fava when all the following are true:
 1. Fava is running in a Docker container 
 2. Fava is running with a production HTTP server (I tried the default cheroot and also tried running with gunicorn), not reproducible running Fava with the Flask dev server

I've [narrowed it down to the watcher thread](https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/7fc834f2020a86244cb5709b0757ebdc0cd2613f/src/fava/core/__init__.py#L284):
```
        if not self._is_encrypted:
            self.watcher.update(*self.paths_to_watch())
```

Simply deleting that line makes the hanging go away.

I can trace the hanging to [pre-request initialization (`fava.application._setup_filters._pull_beancount_file`, where `FavaLedger` is created)](https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/7fc834f2020a86244cb5709b0757ebdc0cd2613f/src/fava/application.py#L231).

My initial suspicion is that this is some kind of subtle deadlock/race condition because it only appears in certain circumstances. My Python-specific multi-threading experience is limited, so I don't know all the tools available, but I'm going to keep poking.

To reproduce in a Docker container, here's a Dockerfile:

```
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
FROM python:3.10-slim
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED 1

RUN apt update && apt install -y git npm

RUN python -m venv /opt/venv
ENV VIRTUAL_ENV="/opt/venv"
ENV PATH="/opt/venv/bin:$PATH"

WORKDIR /bean
RUN mkdir -p beancount smart_importer fava

RUN git clone --branch v2 https://github.com/beancount/beancount.git
RUN git clone https://github.com/beancount/fava.git

RUN pip install ./beancount
RUN pip install ./fava[dev]

# Uncomment these to override what gets packaged into the image (to experiment with local changes)
# Make sure that these are these same as HEAD + intended local changes).
# COPY fava/src/fava/core/watcher.py /opt/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/fava/core/watcher.py
# COPY fava/src/fava/core/__init__.py /opt/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/fava/core/__init__.py

RUN bean-example > /example.bean
CMD ["fava", "/example.bean", "--port", "5000", "--host", "0.0.0.0"]
```
issue_comments:
Searching online, I found `pystack` and I ran `pystack remote ${FAVA_PID} --locals --no-block` before any requests came in (to see the idle state) and after. Here are my main findings:

First, there is a new thread running the watcher (as expected). 

```
Traceback for thread 118 (fava) [Has the GIL] (most recent call last):
    (Python) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/threading.py", line 973, in _bootstrap
        self._bootstrap_inner()
      Arguments:
        self: <_WatchfilesThread at 0x733bae5000d0>
    (Python) File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/threading.py", line 1016, in _bootstrap_inner
        self.run()
      Arguments:
        self: <_WatchfilesThread at 0x733bae5000d0>
    (Python) File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/fava/core/watcher.py", line 60, in run
        for changes in watch(
      Arguments:
        self: <_WatchfilesThread at 0x733bae5000d0>
      Locals:
        watch_paths: <set at 0x733bcc04a260>
    (Python) File "/opt/venv/lib/python3.10/site-packages/watchfiles/main.py", line 118, in watch
        with RustNotify(
      Locals:
        recursive: True
        poll_delay_ms: 300
        raise_interrupt: True
        debug: False
        paths: (<PosixPath at 0x733bcc0ed3c0>)
        ignore_permission_denied: True
        yield_on_timeout: False
        rust_timeout: 5000
        force_polling: False
        step: 50
        stop_event: <Event at 0x733bae551750>
        debounce: 1600
        watch_filter: <method at 0x733bcfb17000>
```

What is surprising is that it has the GIL. From [the watchfiles documentation](https://watchfiles.helpmanual.io/#how-watchfiles-works):

> watchfiles is based on the [Notify](https://github.com/notify-rs/notify) rust library.
> 
> All the hard work of integrating with the OS's file system events notifications and falling back to polling is palmed off onto the rust library.

It seems like in some conditions (running in a Docker container with cheroot), watchfiles either hangs onto the GIL when there are no changes or the actual call to `RustNotify` hangs (which causes the GIL never to be released). I'm suspecting it's the latter, because I tried adding a `print("Saw change")` to the `for changes in watch(...):` loop in in `fava.core.watcher` and then (after fava seemed to have hung) changing an innocuous line in `/example.bean`, but fava seemed to make no progress. So even if `watchfiles` is just hanging on to the GIL, you'd expect that "Saw change" would be printed. Instead, given that nothing happens, I suspect that `RustNotify` is hanging.

The only other changes to the `pystack` output were the thread where fava code was being executed (it seemed to be stuck on one of the `print()` lines I added to debug) and the main thread (which was running cheroot code and it now had a couple of new local variables: `new_conn` and a `sock_fd`).

All of this is making me wonder what is happening in `watchfiles`, especially inside `RustNotify`. Will investigate more when I get a chance. I'll also look into seeing whether it's possible to switch to pure-Python filesystem polling, which might be more inefficient but at least we'll know that the thread will release the GIL.

Gah. This was much less interesting than I thought!

I was able to fix this by passive `False` for [`recursive` in `watchfiles.watch`](https://github.com/samuelcolvin/watchfiles/blob/9f19a18da5323d0e63a2ee16c47ed6c819f89be5/watchfiles/main.py#L101-L102) in [fava code](https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/7fc834f2020a86244cb5709b0757ebdc0cd2613f/src/fava/core/watcher.py#L51-L56). Which made me want to check if `recursive` is somehow operating on the parent directory rather than the file itself. I can't find the code that does this, but it definitely looks like this is the behavior. Moving the file to a different directory (e.g., `mv /example.bean /bean/data/example.bean`) fixed the problem.

It's not yet clear to me if it actually makes sense for fava to switch to `recursive=False` for everyone (since I assume the only paths being passed in are files and not directories), but at least I have a quick fix.

Oh, the confusing behavior happens because of [the following line](https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/7fc834f2020a86244cb5709b0757ebdc0cd2613f/src/fava/core/watcher.py#L49C9-L49C76):

```py
watch_paths = {f.parent for f in self._file_set} | self._folder_set
```

If this is the intended behavior, then it kind of makes sense that the server would come to halt as soon as we try to watch `/`. Not sure how to prevent others from facing the same problem as I have, but I also don't have any suggestion on what fava could be doing differently.

I guess the only reasonable thing I can think of is to print a warning when watching a directory that transitively contains more than a fixed number (e.g., 1,000) files, which seems hard to implement, or when the directory is one of a few known directories (e.g., `/` on *nix and `C:/` on Windows), but that seems like a terrible band-aid for this problem.

I'm going to close this issue. @yagebu (who is, to my knowledge, the primary maintainer of fava), feel free to re-open this if you think it's worthy of doing something about.

Thanks for reporting and debugging this.

I think this is something we should fix - the recursive watching of the parent directory of the beancount file is an unintended side effect of the fix for #1778. `watchfiles` only allows specifying whether to recursively watch once - and for the document directories that's something we want. Simplest way to fix this is to span watchfiles separately for files and folders.

issue_title: Rendering commas or setting locale breaks precision
issue_body:
Using Beancount ~2.3.5~ 2.3.6 and Fava 1.26.1

If Beancount's `render_commas` is `True` or Fava's `locale` is set, the precision is ignored and the account balance is shown with the wrong precision.

Minimal example file:

```
option "operating_currency" "ARS"
;option "render_commas" "TRUE"
2023-01-01 custom "fava-option" "locale" "es_AR"

2023-01-01 commodity ARS
    name: "Argentine Peso ($)"
    precision: 2

2023-01-01 open Assets:Broker:XXX                          XXX
2023-01-01 open Assets:Cash                                ARS

2023-01-01 * "Buy some XXX"
    Assets:Broker:XXX                              6164.93 XXX {18.792541616 ARS}
    Assets:Cash

2023-01-02 balance Assets:Broker:XXX               6164.93 XXX
```

## Result

The balance shows `115.854,704`.
The same occurs if `locale` is commented out and `render_commas` is uncommented.

http://127.0.0.1:5000/locale-format/balance_sheet/
![imagen](https://github.com/beancount/fava/assets/6282941/70a292c3-484b-4ab5-8c5e-12c17870c560)

## Expected result

The balance should display `115.854,70`

If I don't set a `locale` and set `render_commas` to `False`, the balance is correcly rounded (but of course then I lose the thousands separator, and get the wrong decimal separator for my language).

![imagen](https://github.com/beancount/fava/assets/6282941/d3af1e29-c1fe-4cf5-bbcb-2eedc0b42997)
issue_comments:

issue_title: 1.26 regression: Account listings with umlaut are now empty
issue_body:
A second regression I noticed in 1.26: When an account name includes a German umlaut, the account page is now empty. For example:

`http://localhost:2020/<my-filename>/account/Aufw%C3%A4nde:30-Haushalt/`

Both the table and the charts are empty. The heading (blue bar at the top) shows `Aufw%C3%A4nde:30-Haushalt` instead of `Aufwände:30-Haushalt` as the account name. Maybe the account name is just URL-encoded too early. Or it’s never URL-decoded.

Also, on the balance sheet and income statement, these accounts show up just fine with their correct numbers. Only the `/account/` page appears to be broken.

I have tested an account named "Jahresüberschuss" and two whole classes of accounts starting with "Erträge:" and "Aufwände:". When I remove the ü/ä from the account names, Fava works fine.

Version 1.25 did not have this problem. The dev tools console in Firefox shows no messages. I have just verifed the bug with Fava 1.25 and Fava 1.26 right after each other with the same Firefox, so apparently it’s not browser behavior.
issue_comments: I can confirm this as well; my accounts containing Swedish letters 'åäö' is now broken in the account page.
Same here using åäö in account names. Tested in several different browsers in macOS, iOS and Windows with the same result. Downgraded back to 1.25 and the problem is gone.

I managed to get the account reports working by changing [this line](https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/216d7ddf5796dd46f12515e8c2726692393e545b/frontend/src/reports/accounts/index.ts#L21). Or rather the corresponding line in the compiled .js-file. Where the account variable was actually called account2... I won't even pretend to understand how web development works :sweat_smile: 

From:
```
      a: account,
```
To:
```
      a: decodeURI(account),
```

This gets the account page working again, but the account name is still url-encoded in the heading as @MJochim described, so this is not the complete fix, but hopefully it helps someone with better understanding of this stuff to come up with a proper fix.

@fredrik-eriksson Based on what you did I found out how to fix both the heading and the journal. I know how web development works, in general, but I’m not familiar with Svelte. Also, I don't know if @yagebu has any plans of bigger changes to this code, but I’ll prepare a PR and we’ll see if he likes it.
Thanks for the report and for providing the fix :)

issue_title: Editor auto-indentation behavior changed in 1.24
issue_body:
I have noticed a change in the indent behavior starting with v1.24.  In v1.23 and earlier, pressing enter after the first line of the transaction directive would indent the posting lines that follow, until pressing enter on a whitespace line, then it would return to not being indented (set up for the next transaction).  This was very ergonomic for entering multiple transactions in the editor.

Starting with v1.24, indentation is not performed for the first posting line and has to be done manually.  Additional posting lines remain indented, but pressing enter on a whitespace line does not stop indentation and the indent must be manually removed for the next transaction.

This started with commit 3703c9e when dependency [codemirror/language](https://github.com/codemirror/language) upgraded from 6.3.0 to 6.3.1.  The relevant code in CodeMirror is https://github.com/codemirror/language/commit/bd34cf40cb00f1658dba935a2bd0f546ce151bf4 in response to https://github.com/codemirror/dev/issues/1000.  Starting with 6.3.1, codemirror/language treats a `null` response from an indenting service as "I don't know how to indent this and stop trying."  Because of that, when `getIndentation` calls the default indent service implementation in CodeMirror, it gives up on the first null it gets back and does not attempt to ask the `beancountIndent` implementation.

I tried to fix this by implementing `indent()` method in `beancountStreamParser` to simply return `undefined`, but the `StreamParser` interface in CodeMirror doesn't include `undefined` as an acceptable return type so this fails linting.

I'm not sure if CodeMirror needs to do something different but I'm not familiar enough with their project yet to suggest what.  I have pinned codemirror/language to 6.3.0 in package.json for my own build of fava and have not encountered any issues.  I'm not sure if that's acceptable in the short term for Fava, so figured I'd open an issue before submitting a PR.
issue_comments:
Hi, thank you for reporting this and for already debugging the issue!

There might be a way to work around this with the stream parser, but IMHO this should maybe the final push for us to add and use a Lezer parser. I've pinned the package for now as suggested by you :)

issue_title: Inconsistencies with price usage in different views
issue_body:
I found some issues with how Fava uses prices provided with `price`  directive depending on the view. Let's consider the following journal with pricing provided for 4 consecutive days.
```
option "operating_currency" "USD"

2022-01-01 open Assets:Commodity COM
2022-01-01 open Assets:Cash USD
2022-01-01 open Income:All USD

2022-01-01 * "Transfer"
  Assets:Cash                                           100 USD
  Income:All                                           -100 USD

2022-01-01 * "Buy"
  Assets:Cash                                          -100 USD
  Assets:Commodity                                        1 COM {{100 USD}} 
  
2022-01-01 price COM                                    101 USD
2022-01-02 price COM                                    102 USD
2022-01-03 price COM                                    103 USD
2022-01-04 price COM                                    104 USD
```
In the balance sheet view (at market value) prices seem to be offset 1 day forward. In that case price dated 2022-01-04 (104 USD) is taken to calculate asset values dated 2022-01-05. Also, there is no data point for the purchase date at all.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29689324/207139106-08c0790e-8424-4a06-82d3-df4f98d3f96a.png)

In the account view (balances daily) the offset seems to be in the opposite direction, 1 day backward. Purchase day balance (2022-01-01) is calculated with pricing from the following day (2022-01-02). Pricing from the last available day is used twice (for 2022-01-03 and 2022-01-04).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29689324/207139529-b7bade3e-48f1-4d8b-bd41-5db6b6a93ebf.png)

Is it a fava bug, or is there something I am missing in terms of how pricing data is being used?

I am using beancount 2.3.5 and fava 1.23.
issue_comments:
Hi, thanks for reporting this issue/these issues :)

This looks like some off-by-one errors (when aggregating reports by intervals, the interval ends are always exclusive, so e.g. 2020-01-01 until 2020-02-01 for monthly reports - however to get the prices and to report that to the frontend, we usually need the inclusive end dates for)

issue_title: Race condition in filters corrupts ledger.end_date value
issue_body:
I have an extension that makes use of `ledger.end_date`
However the end_date gets corrupted (specifically, the `value` gets set to `None`) every time the `changed` endpoint is called.  Since the application is threaded, this means that the end_date is corrupted during the extension processing.  I'm not sure what a proper fix for this would look like, since multiple requests from different browsers could change the end_date parameter at any time, so I think the current implementation is fundamentally unsafe, and filters should be preserved in thread-local variables.

However, with a single browser connection, this can be made to behave sanely via:
```
--- a/src/fava/application.py
+++ b/src/fava/application.py
@@ -209,11 +209,15 @@ def template_context() -> dict[str, FavaLedger]:

 @app.before_request
 def _perform_global_filters() -> None:
+    if request.endpoint in ('json_api.get_changed', 'json_api.get_errors'):
+        return
     ledger = getattr(g, "ledger", None)
     if ledger:
         # check (and possibly reload) source file
         if request.blueprint != "json_api":
             ledger.changed()
```
issue_comments:
Hey, thanks for the report. I was somewhat aware of the danger of mutating the ledger here but in the early single-threaded days it wasn't a problem and then I was too lazy to do anything about it. The complete fix for this that I am working on is to generate a new object (the filtered ledger) in each report (with some caching) instead of mutating the ledger itself.

I've also added your patch in #1429 in the meantime.

issue_title: Change default port (at least on macOS)
issue_body:
macOS now uses port 5000 by default for AirPlay discovery (https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=29006861).
Attempting to start Fava with the default port now fails silently on macOS (it says it's running, but the web interface doesn't load because the port is already in use by the system).

Port 5000 is in any case already a reserved port for UPnP:
- https://www.iana.org/assignments/service-names-port-numbers/service-names-port-numbers.xhtml?&page=88
- https://www.grc.com/port_5000.htm

It would be better to use a port in the 8000+ range (and also to detect if the port is already in use and fall back to a higher port).
issue_comments:
Thanks for the report :) 

This port was chosen simply by being the default port that the Flask dev server binds on. I think I'll stick with that default like Flask (https://github.com/pallets/flask/issues/4328), but Fava should definitely error out if the port is already in use. It seems that it currently doesn't due to cheroot binding to ip6 localhost (so [::1]:5000) if ip4 localhost is in use (which I find to be surprising behaviour). I'll fix that.
Encountered this issue today. Seems like the error handling in #1378 may not be working.
A PR with a fix would be welcome then :)

You can just add an alias in the `~/.zshrc` file to set the port to a different one. I don't have issues with it on MacOS.

```
alias startfava="fava main.beancount -p 5001"
```

You can then just type `startfava` in your Terminal. Edit the port and file to your needs

I used python default web server to bind the loopback ipv4 and ipv6 on port 5000:
```
➜ python3 -m http.server 5000
Serving HTTP on 0.0.0.0 port 5000 (http://0.0.0.0:5000/) ...
``` 
(and in another terminal)
```
➜ python3 -m http.server -b ::1 5000
Serving HTTP on ::1 port 5000 (http://[::1]:5000/) ...
```

let's see what tcp port is open:
```
➜ ss -tln
State                  Recv-Q                 Send-Q                                 Local Address:Port                                 Peer Address:Port
LISTEN                 0                      5                                          127.0.0.1:5000                                      0.0.0.0:*
LISTEN                 0                      511                                        127.0.0.1:6463                                      0.0.0.0:*
LISTEN                 0                      5                                              [::1]:5000                                         [::]:*
```
We can see that both ipv4 and ipv6 address are in use for port 5000

then I started fava 

``` 
➜ uv run src/fava/cli.py  tests/data/query-example.beancount
Starting Fava on http://127.0.0.1:5000
Error: Cannot start Fava because port 5000 is already in use.
Please choose a different port with the '-p' option.
```

*The error is expected because both ipv4 and ipv6 address are in use for port 5000.*

Then I closed all my terminal sessions.

I started only the ipv4 python server this time:
```
➜ python3 -m http.server -b 127.0.0.1 5000
Serving HTTP on 127.0.0.1 port 5000 (http://127.0.0.1:5000/) ...
```

let's the listening ports:
```
➜ ss -tln
State                  Recv-Q                 Send-Q                                 Local Address:Port                                 Peer Address:Port
LISTEN                 0                      5                                          127.0.0.1:5000                                      0.0.0.0:*
LISTEN                 0                      511                                        127.0.0.1:6463                                      0.0.0.0:*
```
only the ipv4 one as expected

and when we run fava:
```
➜ uv run src/fava/cli.py  tests/data/query-example.beancount
Starting Fava on http://127.0.0.1:5000
Error: Cannot start Fava because port 5000 is already in use.
Please choose a different port with the '-p' option.
```
*I get the expected error.*

I am testing this on a linux machine so on Macos the result might be different. But I would be very surprised that it is the case...

Unless someone can successfully reproduce this on a Mac I think this can be closed.

issue_title: Incorrect price dates are used for interval charts.
issue_body: Confusing: Change the date range changes the data in the report (7 and 9), but not the data in the chart (always 7) ------- If you change the price on 06-02 to 06-01, then it is used in the report for month 05.
issue_comments:
The first example works as expected (and I think the current behaviour is sensible): By default, the latest price is used for conversions except for places where we have a date to do the conversion on. So for example for the interval charts, we have the end dates of the interval as canonical conversion dates and in the Journal, the transaction dates are used.

The second one is a bug. The intervals in Beancount all have exclusive end dates, but for the conversion we need to use the inclusive end dates. For the net worth chart, this was already addressed in #973

I have a similar issue:

As an example consider the following entry 

```
2017-01-01      *       "transaction"
        Income:Sales    10 USD @ 1.2 CAD
        Assets:Savings

2017-01-02 price USD 1.5 CAD
```

In Fava, the assets would (correctly) show as 12 CAD, but the income statement would show sales and net profit of 15 CAD in the "Converted to CAD" mode. The two reports - assets and income do not balance with themselves. I think that since the conversion value has been explicitly specified in the posting, that value should be considered by Fava instead of the latest conversion value. 

If I have 100 USD in my bank account (Asset), their value in terms of CAD will keep on changing every month and so the latest conversion value should be used - that makes sense. But if I made a sale (Income) of 100 USD and realised the proceedings in CAD with the exchange rate that I specified, then I think the conversion should be made with that exchange rate and not with the latest available rate.

Further, I think it is wrong to use the latest price for conversions for income and expense accounts at all.

Let's say you are a Canadian company that made a sale of 1 million USD and deposited it to your Canadian account when the prevailing rate was 1 USD = 1.2 CAD, hence getting 1.2 million CAD in your account. At the end of the year when you make your annual report, the prevailing rate has increased to 1 USD = 1.5 CAD. Fava would show your annual sales as 1.5 million CAD instead of 1.2 million CAD. That is clearly incorrect and you wouldn't show 1.5 million CAD in your annual income report or tax returns when you made only 1.2 million CAD. 

Let's take another example where you are the same Canadian company and you again make a sale of 1 million USD but this time deposit it to a US bank account instead of a Canadian bank account. In this case what Fava reports might make some sense, as by the end of the year the 1 million USD that you made as income would now be worth 1.5 million CAD. But I would argue that even in this case it should not be reported as such as the difference (0.3 million CAD) is unrealised currency gain and should be reported towards a currency gain account, and not towards the sales account which is being done right now.

I think that income and expense accounts are "realised" accounts, as in when you book an income, the amount that you make gets realised at the date of the transaction. And that for these accounts any currency conversion must be carried out according to the rates applicable for the date of the transaction, and not the current date or the last date of the interval. I think that asset accounts and income/expense accounts are fundamentally different in this way.

maybe for "market value" and "currency" conversions, an option to say when the conversion happens should be given, and a user can select "at posting time", "at aggreation end" (for bar charts), "at the end of the reporting period (not know if this is useful?)

issue_title: Cannot type date into "Time" on Safari
issue_body:
# Summary

When trying to type custom date range like "2017-01" into the "Time" input filter in the upper right corner, the main content does no update accordingly. However, directly changing URL with parameter "time=2017-01" updates the content.

Besides, this bug only happens on Safari, but not on Chrome or Firefox.

# Steps to reproduce on Safari:
0. Open Safari
1. Go to [demo page](https://fava.pythonanywhere.com/example-with-budgets/income_statement/)
2. Type "2017-01" in the "Time" input box
3. Press Enter.

# Current Behavior:

The content does **not** change, looks like the date filter "2017-01" is not propagate correctly.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1069722/81451357-2cdbff00-9139-11ea-9191-94592cc07b15.png)


# Expected Behavior:

The content change to show only time for Jan, 2017:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1069722/81451282-fc946080-9138-11ea-80c0-23814fdaed1d.png)

# Versions
* Safari version: `Version 13.1 (15609.1.20.111.8)`
* OS: `10.15.4 (19E287)`
issue_comments:
Thanks for the bug report. From an initial glance, the spec seems to be pretty clear on how an Enter key press should be interpreted in form. Unless this is due to some other frontend bug that we only run into on Safari, it might be a bug in Safari.

I don't have a Mac so I can't really debug this. Could you try debugging this a bit? (Opening the developer console and looking for errors there would be a start).

Still can't reproduce this for obvious reasons - but I'll address it by also triggering the submit on the `blur` event, that is, when focus leaves the input. That should ensure that inputs and filters are always in sync.

issue_title: Autocompletion fails on non-transaction directives
issue_body:
Autocompletion throws a TypeError every time it's not used in a transaction.

How to reproduce: type `1970-01-01 open A`. It should hint `Assets` accounts, but instead it throws the following error:

    Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'filter' of undefined
        at fuzzyMatch (app.js?mtime=1558219766:22610)
        at app.js?mtime=1558219766:22716
        at fetchHints (app.js?mtime=1558219766:22160)
        at run (app.js?mtime=1558219766:22173)
        at resolved (app.js?mtime=1558219766:22178)
        at fetchHints (app.js?mtime=1558219766:22158)
        at Completion.update (app.js?mtime=1558219766:21899)
        at CodeMirror.showHint (app.js?mtime=1558219766:21828)
        at Object.CodeMirror.showHint [as autocomplete] (app.js?mtime=1558219766:21802)
        at app.js?mtime=1558219766:23228

I have been taking a look at the code and the problem seems to be in [hint-beancount.js:105](https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/master/fava/static/javascript/codemirror/hint-beancount.js#L105). There, we are passing an undefined, e.g. `completionSources["accounts"]` (?) into `fuzzyMatch`, and instead we should be passing the list of accounts or commodities.
issue_comments: Thanks for finding this bug and providing a fix for it. I wonder if that code ever made sense...

issue_title: Keyboard entry filtering doesn't work on safari.
issue_body:
Creating issue based on @victoraraujo01 's comment in https://github.com/beancount/fava/issues/768#issuecomment-403682834

When entering in a filter in safari (e.g. year), the entry doesn't seem to work when entered via keyboard. 
![beancount_fava_safari_filter_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/297435/44820004-e41e2580-aba3-11e8-9153-8fe757693d42.gif)
issue_comments: Does the browser console show any errors?

Browser console doesn't appear to have any errors. 
Is there anywhere else I could set a breakpoint for besides `e.on('form-submit-filters' ...` in app.js ?

Humm, okay it looks like the general event listener (line 314 of app.js, or line 13 of helpers.js https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/b6f65541b2538414d761b5a49002da3da6d0f349/fava/static/javascript/helpers.js#L13 ) will trigger on clicking of the element, but not on hitting enter.

Since this works in Firefox and Chrome, I assume this a browser bug in Safari. I can't really investigate this any further, if you can and find some workaround, I'd be happy to merge that. Otherwise this would be a wontfix to me.
Yep, fine with it being a wontfix. Spent a little bit of time trying to figure out how to solve this but it's not a priority at the moment - just been using another browser instead to workaround it.

issue_title: Navigation in editor by arrow keys is interrupted by autocomplete popup (focus is lost)
issue_body:
If I move cursor in editor window with keys Up, Down, Home, End, PgUp, PgDown, and cursor happens to stop on word with autocomplete popup, then popup appears and navigation with keys can't be continued: focus is stolen by popup.

Is there any workaround to show popup only by hotkey?
issue_comments: I also experience some weird behavior with the autocomplete popup, like as @Vanav describes.

What we currently do (https://github.com/beancount/fava/blob/master/fava/static/javascript/editor.js#L147) is basically the same as this SE answer: https://stackoverflow.com/a/33021864. There are some other answers that suggest excluding more keys. Any help on fixing this would be very welcome and should be easy! (If you've got any questions on how to get started, just ask)
@Vanav I implemented a fix in PR #639 - Can you test this code and report back if it works as you expect/fixes this behavior?
@aumayr, ah, I've did similar fix a day ago, just was testing internally: PR #641. Now you can choose one or both.

issue_title: Net worth disagrees with bean-report
issue_body: When I run bean-report networth I get different numbers in my two currencies than from fava.  The bean-report numbers are higher and closer to what I was seeing about a month ago with fava.  My guess is something changed.
issue_comments:
This is not good. Can you provide a sample file where this is happening?

(Ref #126)
I don't know that I can do this in the next week or so... I'm about to go on vacation.  Otherwise I would have given more information and done some debugging ;)
I just saw this too. I couldn't quickly come up with a minimal example of this bug. But it might be related to cost/prices.

I have two currencies (INR and USD), and fava matches with beancount's report exactly on INR. On USD, it's way off. It's reporting less than half of what beancount reports. 
The calculation of the net worth is the last place where we do basic arithmetic by hand, which we shouldn't do and which might lead to this bug. I'll rewrite the code to use `beancount`'s functions, that should hopefully fix it.
Can you try my branch [net-worth](https://github.com/yagebu/fava/tree/net-worth) to see if that changes anything?
